topic: Practical OpenAI and ChatGPT usage patterns, real use cases

entries.q: What is the OpenAI Cookbook and where can I find it?
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook is a resource for example code and guides on using the OpenAI API, and it can be found at cookbook.openai.com.

entries.q: What do I need to run the examples in the OpenAI Cookbook?
entries.a: To run the examples, you need an OpenAI account and an associated API key.

entries.q: How can I set my OpenAI API key as an environment variable?
entries.a: You can set an environment variable called OPENAI_API_KEY with your API key, or create an .env file at the root of your repository containing OPENAI_API_KEY=<your API key>.

entries.q: In what programming language are most OpenAI Cookbook code examples written?
entries.a: Most code examples in the OpenAI Cookbook are written in Python.

entries.q: Are the concepts in the OpenAI Cookbook limited to Python?
entries.a: No, the concepts in the OpenAI Cookbook can be applied in any programming language.

entries.q: What type of license does the OpenAI Cookbook use?
entries.a: The OpenAI Cookbook uses the MIT License.

entries.q: Where can I find related resources and courses for OpenAI and the API?
entries.a: Related resources and courses can be found on the OpenAI Cookbook website, at cookbook.openai.com/related_resources.

entries.q: How do I create a free OpenAI account to get started with the API?
entries.a: You can create a free OpenAI account by signing up at platform.openai.com/signup.
